relationship_detail: AT&T Inc. owns AT&T Mobility LLC (wireless operating subsidiary)
AT&T Inc. owns Cricket Wireless LLC (acquired via Leap Wireless 2014, $1.19B)
AT&T acquired FiberTower 24 GHz spectrum holdings
AT&T holds 25-year FirstNet public safety network contract ($6.5B initial + $8B expansion)
Verizon Communications owns Cellco Partnership (d/b/a Verizon Wireless)
Verizon acquired Straight Path Communications for $3.1B (2018) for mmWave spectrum
Verizon acquired TracFone Wireless from America Movil for $6.25B (Nov 2021)
Verizon owns Visible Service LLC (digital-first prepaid brand)
Verizon acquired Starry Group Holdings post-bankruptcy
Deutsche Telekom AG is majority owner of T-Mobile US Inc.
T-Mobile owns Metro by T-Mobile (acquired MetroPCS via reverse takeover 2013)
T-Mobile acquired Sprint Corporation for $26B (completed April 2020)
T-Mobile controls Clearwire Corporation (via Sprint merger, holds 2.5 GHz spectrum)
EchoStar completed acquisition of Dish Network Dec 31, 2023
EchoStar owns Dish Wireless LLC (wireless operations)
EchoStar owns Hughes Network Systems LLC (satellite broadband)
EchoStar owns Boost Mobile (acquired from Sprint/T-Mobile merger divestiture)
FCC found Dish Network maintains de facto control of Northstar Wireless LLC despite claimed independence. $3.3B bidding credits denied.
FCC found Dish Network maintains de facto control of SNR Wireless LicenseCo LLC. D.C. Circuit upheld 2022.
Dish Network controls ParkerB.com Wireless LLC (spectrum holding shell)
Dish Network controls Weminuche LLC (spectrum holding shell)
Dish Network controls Wetterhorn Wireless LLC (spectrum holding shell)
Dish Network controls Crestone Peak Wireless LLC (spectrum holding shell)
Dish Network controls Maroon Bells Wireless LLC (spectrum holding shell)
Comcast operates Xfinity Mobile (MVNO brand launched 2017)
Charter Communications operates Spectrum Mobile (MVNO brand launched 2018)
Cox Enterprises Inc. owns Cox Communications Inc.
Grain Management owns NewLevel LLC (600 MHz spectrum portfolio)
Grain Management owns NewLevel II LP (C-band spectrum portfolio, $1.277B at Auction 107)
Columbia Capital backs Three Forty-Five Spectrum LLC ($1.4B at Auction 110)
Columbia Capital owned AB Spectrum LLC (700 MHz A Block, sold to T-Mobile 2016)
Columbia Capital financed Channel 51 License Co LLC ($859M in 600 MHz auction)
Rivada Networks wholly owns Rivada Space Networks GmbH (satellite constellation subsidiary)
Harbinger Capital Partners (Philip Falcone) original backer of LightSquared/Ligado, invested ~$2.9B. Retained minority post-bankruptcy.
Centerbridge Partners: major post-bankruptcy owner of Ligado Networks (2015 restructuring)
Fortress Investment Group: major post-bankruptcy owner of Ligado Networks (2015 restructuring)
NextNav Inc. owns Progeny LMS LLC (M-LMS licensee in 902-928 MHz)
Telephone and Data Systems Inc. is parent of US Cellular Corporation
SoftBank Group owns Fortress Investment Group LLC
IDT Corporation spun off Straight Path Communications in 2013. Straight Path later sold to Verizon.
America Movil sold TracFone Wireless to Verizon for $6.25B (2021)
Rivada Networks is JV partner in Rivada Port Graham Solutions LLC (DHS TACCOM contractor)
Port Graham Corporation (Alaska Native Corp) is JV partner in Rivada Port Graham Solutions LLC
Dish Network partnered with Doyon Limited (Alaska Native Corp) via Northstar Wireless for designated entity bidding in AWS-3 auction
Comcast was partner in SpectrumCo LLC (AWS-1 spectrum JV with TWC and Bright House)
EchoStar sold $23B in spectrum (600 MHz + 3.45 GHz) to AT&T in 2025
EchoStar sold spectrum to SpaceX as part of $42.6B total divestiture
UScellular sold wireless operations and ~30% of spectrum to T-Mobile for $4.4B (2024-2025)
UScellular sold spectrum to AT&T for $1.018B (2024)
UScellular sold ~$1B in spectrum to Verizon (2024)
Comcast sold $3.3B in 600 MHz spectrum to T-Mobile (2023), pivoting to CBRS
SpectrumCo LLC sold AWS-1 spectrum to Verizon for $3.6B (2012), enabling cable MVNO agreements
Sprint/T-Mobile divested 800 MHz spectrum ($3.6B) and Boost Mobile ($1.4B) to Dish as merger condition
Grain Management acquired 800 MHz from T-Mobile and returned 600 MHz licenses T-Mobile had been leasing (2025)
AB Spectrum (Columbia Capital) sold 700 MHz A Block licenses to T-Mobile (2016)
Verizon provides network for Comcast Xfinity Mobile under MVNO agreement (originated from SpectrumCo deal)
Verizon provides network for Charter Spectrum Mobile under MVNO agreement
Verizon provides network for Cox Mobile under MVNO agreement
AT&T hosts EchoStar/Boost Mobile as MVNO after Dish 5G network shutdown (Nov 2025)
In-Q-Tel (CIA venture arm) was seed investor in Palantir Technologies
Peter Thiel is investor in both Palantir Technologies and Rivada Networks — linking IC analytics to spectrum ambitions
Lockheed Martin GPS III program opposed Ligado Networks L-band terrestrial operations as threat to GPS signal integrity
Raytheon radar systems depend on spectrum bands adjacent to commercial allocations; advocated for federal spectrum sharing not vacating
Original SAIC split into SAIC (government services) and Leidos (defense/health IT) in 2013
L3Harris formed from merger of L3 Technologies and Harris Corporation (June 2019)
Alaska Native Wireless was financed by AT&T to bid on set-aside spectrum in Auction 35. Called a "shill" for AT&T by competitors.
Salmon PCS backed by Cingular Wireless (later AT&T) to bid on set-aside spectrum. Called a "shill" for incumbent carriers.
Northstar Wireless used Doyon Limited (Alaska Native Corp) as designated entity partner. FCC found Dish maintained de facto control.
Charter Communications and Cox Communications announced definitive merger agreement (2025)
Rivada Space Networks formed proxy organization (separate from Rivada) to serve classified government/defense satellite customers
Iridium urged FCC to reverse Ligado L-band approval due to interference concerns with adjacent satellite operations
Viasat and L3Harris both provide tactical military communications systems; compete and partner on DoD programs
Lumen and AT&T both operate major internet backbone networks used by government/defense customers
DigitalBridge acquired Boingo Wireless for $854M (2021)
Apple invested $1.5B in Globalstar for satellite SOS iPhone capability
Sprint Spectrum operated through WirelessCo LP bidding entity in Auction 4
WirelessCo was Sprint-TCI-Comcast-Cox consortium
Bell Atlantic was one of four PCS PrimeCo partners
NYNEX was one of four PCS PrimeCo partners
AirTouch was one of four PCS PrimeCo partners
US West was one of four PCS PrimeCo partners
Bell Atlantic Mobile became Verizon Wireless 2000
GTE Mobilnet merged into Verizon Wireless 2000
AirTouch merged with Vodafone, then JV with Bell Atlantic 2000
PCS PrimeCo dissolved into Verizon Wireless
McCaw acquired by AT&T 1994 for $11.5B
AT&T Wireless acquired by Cingular 2004 for $41B
Western PCS/VoiceStream common ownership (John Stanton)
Omnipoint acquired by VoiceStream/T-Mobile 2000
Aerial acquired by VoiceStream/T-Mobile 2000
PowerTel acquired by Deutsche Telekom/T-Mobile 2001
VoiceStream became T-Mobile 2001
Nextel merged with Sprint 2005
NextWave C Block spectrum ultimately sold to Verizon, AT&T, MetroPCS
NextWave sold spectrum assets after emerging from bankruptcy
Alaska native corporation entities, DE benefits
Both were C Block DE carriers competing in same markets
Comcast was Sprint PCS cable consortium member
Cox was Sprint PCS cable consortium member
TCI was Sprint PCS cable consortium member
Both LMDS winners, both went bankrupt 2001-2002
Both LMDS winners, both went bankrupt
Both fixed wireless, both Craig McCaw linked
Sirius and XM merged 2008 to form SiriusXM
SWBMS became part of Cingular
BellSouth wireless became part of Cingular
Ameritech wireless (via SBC) became part of Cingular
PacBell wireless (via SBC) became part of Cingular
Cingular became AT&T Mobility after AT&T/BellSouth merger 2007

Grain Management auction vehicle for C-Band Auction 107. Won $1.277B in 10 markets.
Grain Management auction vehicle for 3.45 GHz Auction 110.
Columbia Capital auction vehicle for 3.45 GHz Auction 110. Won $1.4B in 18 licenses across 11 largest markets.
Columbia Capital entity — sold spectrum licenses to T-Mobile US
Columbia Parties hold 19.79% equity in Channel 51 License Co. Channel 51 leases 600 MHz spectrum to T-Mobile.
Moise Advisory/Edward Moise Jr auction vehicle for 3.45 GHz Auction 110. Won 319 licenses.
Moise Advisory/Edward Moise Jr auction vehicle for C-Band Auction 107.
Charles Townsend auction vehicle for C-Band Auction 107 and 3.45 GHz Auction 110.
AT&T acquired FiberTower for 24 GHz and 39 GHz mmWave spectrum. FiberTower was bankrupt 2012, emerged 2014. Crown Castle was early investor.
EchoStar/DISH bidding entity for 700 MHz Auction 73. Won 168 of 176 E block licenses.
Leap/Cricket owned 82.5% of Denali. Won Great Lakes AWS-1 license ($274M net after DE credits). Leap completed Denali acquisition 2010.
Cellular South rebranded to C Spire 2011. Same entity.
Comcast owned 63.6% of SpectrumCo. Cable consortium that won AWS spectrum and sold to Verizon for $3.6B in 2012.
Time Warner Cable (now Charter) owned 31.2% of SpectrumCo.
Verizon acquired Straight Path Communications for $3.1B (2018) for mmWave 28/39 GHz spectrum. Previously IDT subsidiary.
TDS owns 83% of US Cellular
TDS owns 83% of US Cellular
DigitalBridge (fka Digital Colony) acquired Boingo Wireless for $854M in 2021.
Harbinger Capital backed LightSquared/Ligado. Phil Falcone invested heavily in L-Band spectrum.
Sprint PCS joint venture (Sprint/TCI/Comcast/Cox) for Auction 4 PCS A/B blocks. $2.1B in winning bids.
PCS PrimeCo LP — JV of AirTouch, Bell Atlantic, NYNEX, US West. All four parents merged into Verizon. Won $1.1B in PCS licenses.
Alaska Native Corporation designated entity. Used tribal/Alaska Native bidding credits. CIRI is an Alaska Native regional corporation.
AT&T acquired Dobson Communications 2007 for $2.8B. Oklahoma-based rural carrier.
AT&T acquired Centennial Communications 2009. Regional carrier in Louisiana/Indiana/Michigan.
AT&T acquired McCaw Cellular 1994 for $11.5B — Craig McCaw's pioneering cellular company. Became AT&T Wireless.
Aerial Communications acquired by VoiceStream (later T-Mobile) 1999.
APC acquired by Sprint PCS. Washington DC metro PCS carrier.
IDT spun off Straight Path Communications (mmWave spectrum). Straight Path acquired by Verizon 2018 for $3.1B. IDT founded by Howard Jonas.
GWI PCS Inc. rebranded as MetroPCS Communications Inc. after emerging from bankruptcy.
USCC Wireless Investment Inc. (US Cellular sub) is limited partner. Won 124 AWS-3 licenses for $338M.
TDS owns 83% of US Cellular. Carlson family controls TDS.
Doyon Ltd (Alaska Native Corp) held 17.5% controlling interest in Denali Spectrum. Leap/Cricket held 82.5% equity. Same Doyon later connected to DISH's Northstar scheme.
Doyon Ltd connected to both Denali Spectrum (legitimate DE) and Northstar Wireless (DISH DE scheme). Pattern of Alaska Native Corps used as DE vehicles.
Charlie Townsend's spectrum vehicle. Became largest UHF-TV channel owner in US. Sold 700 MHz spectrum to AT&T. Predecessor to Whitewater Wireless.
Shentel acquired NTELOS Holdings (parent of King Street Wireless) in 2016.
AT&T Mobility acquired Aloha Partners for $2.5B (2008). Charlie Townsend's 700 MHz spectrum play — one of most successful auction flips in FCC history.
Columbia Capital mmWave auction vehicle. Auction 103.
Charter subsidiary for CBRS Auction 105 bidding. $464M in 210 PAL licenses.
Comcast subsidiary for CBRS Auction 105. "XF" = Xfinity. $459M in 830 PAL licenses.
